name: cms-opd-pa
description: Medicare FFS hospital-outpatient (POS 22) prior-authorization packet for bilateral endovenous vein ablation (CPT 36475 / 36476) -- a service on the CMS Hospital OPD Required Prior Authorization list (spec-v52 §4.5.2.1). The 'Medicare Part B / Noridian' letterhead routes the bundle to the 'cms-medicare-ffs' bucket, so the §4.5.2 CMS FFS overlay and the new §4.5.2.1 OPD PA membership test (R-PA-OPD-001) engage. The packet requests a listed OPD service in the hospital outpatient department but documents no Unique Tracking Number (UTN) / OPD prior-authorization reference, so R-PA-OPD-001 flags: Medicare requires the OPD prior authorization and the UTN before the service is furnished. The DME-focused R-PA-CMS rules self-gate off (no DME requested) and vacuously pass.

documents.name: cms-opd-form.txt
documents.text:
Medicare Part B Prior Authorization Request (Noridian Healthcare Solutions, MAC Jurisdiction F)
Patient: John Q Public
DOB: 1955-03-08
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier: 1EG4-TE5-MK73
Date of service: 2026-07-15
Place of service: 22
Servicing facility: Mercy Hospital Outpatient Department
Requested procedure: bilateral endovenous radiofrequency vein ablation, great saphenous vein, CPT 36475 and 36476
Dx: I83.ptr811 varicose veins of right lower extremity with ulcer and inflammation
Quantity: 2
Ordering provider NPI: 1234567893
Servicing facility NPI: 1306849393
TIN: 123456789
Medical necessity: symptomatic venous reflux; failed 12 weeks of conservative therapy (compression stockings).
Signature: Vascular Surgery MD, 2026-07-01

documents.name: cms-opd-note.txt
documents.text:
Clinical note
Chief complaint: aching, heaviness, and a non-healing ulcer of the right lower leg.
History of present illness: symptomatic great saphenous vein reflux > 0.5 s on duplex ultrasound; six months of symptoms despite a documented 12-week trial of graduated compression stockings.
Physical exam: visible varicosities medial right calf; healing venous stasis ulcer.
Assessment and plan: endovenous ablation of the right great saphenous vein in the hospital outpatient department.
Medical necessity: CEAP C6; conservative therapy failed.
Note date: 2026-07-01
Signed: Vascular Surgery MD, 2026-07-01
